description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 cloud-architecture cluster: IaC change review vs cloud design skills, and against the shipped secure-migration-reviewer, security-pr-reviewer, and supply-chain-security-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Is this Terraform change safe to apply? It touches subnets, an IAM role, and deletes an S3 lifecycle rule.
cases.reason: A concrete infrastructure-code diff reviewed for apply safety is iac-reviewer's core case — not architecture design, and IaC blast-radius/exposure semantics are its specialization rather than the general security diff lens.

cases.prompt: Check this migration file: it drops a column, rewrites a policy, and grants SELECT to a role.
cases.reason: DDL/DML deploy safety, grants, and RLS policy changes are database-migration review; iac-reviewer owns the infrastructure layer the database runs on.

cases.prompt: Security-review this PR — it changes our API authorization middleware and adds a file-upload endpoint.
cases.reason: Application-code diffs (authz, injection, SSRF surfaces) are security-pr-reviewer; iac-reviewer only reviews infrastructure-as-code.

cases.prompt: Audit our third-party Terraform modules and GitHub Actions for supply-chain risk — pinning, sources, install scripts.
cases.reason: A dedicated supply-chain audit of module/action provenance and pinning is supply-chain-security-reviewer's job; iac-reviewer flags unpinned modules in a diff and routes the systemic audit there.

cases.prompt: Design the network and data architecture our Terraform should implement on Azure.
cases.reason: Designing the target architecture is the provider architect; iac-reviewer reviews changes against that design after it exists.
